P1_Global.narrative: P1 (pooled global model) covers only 1/19 syllable classes with any recall at all (gate: 8/19). Of the 10 target words, only 0 have BOTH syllables inside that covered set -- the remaining 10 are structurally guaranteed wrong at the word level regardless of luck, before any end-to-end pipeline is even built. This is why P1 was excluded from champion selection and never received an end-to-end evaluation.

P2_SubjectDependent_EEGNet.narrative: 12/12 subjects' best P2 (Subject-Dependent EEGNet) configuration fails the same 8/19 class-coverage gate used by champion selection (this is exactly the mechanism that disqualified the P2/S6/E6 accuracy peak in the champion search). An end-to-end pipeline built on a per-subject model that only ever predicts a handful of classes would inherit that collapse directly -- there is no coarse/fine restructuring that fixes a Stage-1 model which never learned most of the class space.

P4_NoWindowing.narrative: P4 (No-Windowing, 5s full epoch) achieves a mean syllable accuracy of 5.98% across 11 subjects, essentially chance level (5.26% for 19 classes -- a ratio of 1.14x). Treating the two syllables of a word as independent draws at this rate gives P(whole word correct) ~ 0.358% -- effectively unusable for a 2-syllable end-to-end pipeline, and likely an OVER-estimate besides, since near-chance accuracy is often concentrated in a single collapsed class (as directly observed for P1) rather than spread with genuine per-class signal. Confirms sample-size scarcity (5s epoch = 1/5 the training windows of the standard 1s-windowed paradigms) as the bottleneck, not windowing structure itself.

P5_ShiftedBandpass.narrative: P5 (Shifted Bandpass 15-65Hz) vs P3's own E0_Baseline/barlow accuracy (same config, fair same-baseline comparison, NOT the E5 champion): mean delta -1.11pp across 12 subjects, 7/12 in the negative direction, Wilcoxon p=0.3013 (not statistically significant) -- this is a 'redundant confirmation' finding (the shifted band carries essentially the same, not more, signal than the standard band), structurally different from P1/P2/P4's 'compounding error from Stage-1 collapse' argument, so no end-to-end pipeline was built for P5 either: there is nothing for a hierarchical or word-assembly stage to add on top of a syllable-classification result that is already statistically indistinguishable from the existing baseline.
